subject: Physics
title: What Is a Black Hole?
description: A quick win: understand What Is a Black Hole?.

hook.text: A black hole is a region of space where gravity is so strong that nothing—not even light—can escape.

buildup.text: When a massive star collapses, its matter compresses into an infinitely dense point called a singularity.

discovery.text: The boundary around a black hole is the event horizon. Cross it and you can never return, even at light speed.

twist.text: From outside, time appears to freeze at the event horizon. A falling astronaut would seem to hang there forever.

climax.text: Black holes don't suck—they attract with gravity like any mass. If the Sun became a black hole, Earth's orbit wouldn't change.

punchline.text: Light goes in but never comes out. The universe's ultimate trap.

quiz.question: What is the event horizon of a black hole?
options: The boundary beyond which nothing can escape
The center where all mass is concentrated
The ring of light around a black hole
